SUITE 7007

LOS ANGELES, 90012 
Mr. Don Rogers 
242 North Arrowhead, Suite 200 
San Bernardino, California 92408 
Dear Mr. Rogers: 
~ 
January 23, 1981 
Pursuant to your request, this letter is to confirm my 
advice to you during our telephone conversation of yesterday, 
Thursday, January 22, 1981. 
Specifically, you asked when does the cumulation period 
end under the 1980 provisions of the Political Reform Act. 
Government Code Section 82018 defines "cumulative amount" 
in part as--the amount contributed or expended since the closing 
date of the most recent post-election statement which was required 
to be filed by the filer. 
The period for which all contributions 
from a single source were to be combined began with the closing 
date of the last post-election statement filed and ended on the 
closing date of the next post-election statement. 
The filing of semi-annual campaign statements during off 
election year periods did not end the obligation to continue 
cumulating receipts and expenditures. 
As a result of legislation passed, signed into law and 
effective as of January 1, 1981, the new provisions concerning 
the "cumulative" amount of receipts and expenditures has been 
changed. 
The new Government Code Section 82018 now states that, 
"cumulative amount means the amount of contributions received 
or expenditures made in a calendar year." 
That is that the 
cumulation period ,.,rill begin on January 1, 1981 and end on 
December 31, 1981. 
I hope this information has been helpful to you. 
Please 
feel free to contact our office for any 
assistance. 
Consultant 
HPA: jg 
SACRAMENTO (916) 322·5660

lOS ANGELES 1213) 620-5196

sections.question: When does the cumulation period end under the 1980 provisions of the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion: The new Government Code Section 82018, effective January 1, 1981, defines 'cumulative amount' as 'the amount of contributions received or expenditures made in a calendar year.' The cumulation period will begin on January 1, 1981 and end on December 31, 1981.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks when the cumulation period ends for contributions and expenditures under the 1980 provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under new legislation effective January 1, 1981, the cumulation period for contributions and expenditures changed from post-election statement closing dates to a calendar year basis (January 1 to December 31).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion. Telephone conversation confirmed in writing. Legislation effective date and specific Government Code section clearly referenced.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: When does the cumulation period end under the 1980 provisions of the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: The new Government Code Section 82018, effective January 1, 1981, defines 'cumulative amount' as 'the amount of contributions received or expenditures made in a calendar year.' The cumulation period will begin on January 1, 1981 and end on December 31, 1981.
